summary: iter26 — POST /api/auth/resend-verification + EmailAuthDialog resend entry points: backend pytest 7/7 PASSED (2.05s), frontend E2E 9/9 checkpoints PASSED against REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL. Backend covers all branches called out in the request (nonexistent → 200 generic, invalid email → 422, already-verified → 200 generic + no DB changes, google account → 200 generic + no DB changes, unverified password user → 200 + verification_token regenerated + verification_expires_at ~24h + last_resend_at set + email_verified unchanged, immediate 2nd call → 429 with 'Por favor espera Xs antes…', last_resend_at older than cooldown → succeeds with new token). Frontend E2E validated all 3 entry points: (a) login-mode footer link 'auth-resend-verification-link' with text '¿No recibiste el correo de verificación?' → empty email shows toast 'Ingresa tu email primero' and makes NO backend call; filled email triggers POST /api/auth/resend-verification → 200 + success toast 'Si la cuenta existe…'; (b) post-register success card shows 'auth-resend-verification-btn' with text '¿No recibiste el correo? Reenviar' → click triggers backend 200 + success toast; (c) EMAIL_NOT_VERIFIED login response renders the same success-state with 'auth-resend-verification-btn' → click hits backend, on second-within-60s returns 429 and the detail message 'Por favor espera 56s antes de solicitar otro correo.' is surfaced as an error toast via axios error path — UX matches spec.
critical_code_review_comments: auth_resend_verification (server.py:904-949) correctly returns the same generic 200 message for nonexistent / already-verified / google-provider users to prevent account enumeration — verified end-to-end. Good security posture.

Rate-limit branch uses datetime.fromisoformat(last_resend.replace('Z','+00:00')); since iso(now_utc()) emits a +00:00 offset, the replace is a no-op but defensive — fine. The except Exception: pass on a malformed date silently bypasses the cooldown — acceptable but worth logging.warning(..) for observability.

Frontend handleResendVerification correctly short-circuits BEFORE any backend call when email is empty (validated: 0 network requests). Resending flag prevents double-submit. The 429 detail is surfaced via toast.error(detail) — UX matches spec.

MINOR (no fix needed): when the EMAIL_NOT_VERIFIED success card is shown and the user clicks Reenviar within 60s of the previous resend, they see the 429 toast — which is informative but could be improved by passing a structured 'cooldown_seconds' instead of parsing detail. Optional.
server.py remains ~3189 lines — extraction into routers/auth.py still recommended (carried over from iter17 review).

success_rate.frontend: 100% (9/9 E2E checkpoints: dialog open, link present, link text, empty-email toast, empty-email NO backend call, filled-email backend 200 + success toast, register success state + btn present + btn text + backend 200, EMAIL_NOT_VERIFIED success state + btn present + backend 429 with cooldown message)

test_credentials: Used fresh ephemeral emails: resend.e2e.<ts>@example.com (login-mode resend link), resend.reg.<ts>@example.com (registered via UI for post-register + EMAIL_NOT_VERIFIED flows). Backend pytest seeds users directly via pymongo (MONGO_URL=mongodb://localhost:27017, DB_NAME=test_database) — same pattern as test_verify_email_iter25.py.
seed_data_creation: Backend tests insert test_resend_<uuid>@example.com users in MongoDB and clean them up in finally blocks. Frontend E2E registered one fresh user (resend.reg.<ts>@example.com) — left in DB unverified; harmless.
context_for_next_testing_agent: iter26 'Reenviar correo de verificación' is FEATURE-COMPLETE backend + frontend. Pytest target: /app/backend/tests/test_resend_verification_iter26.py (7 tests, ~2s). DO NOT run the full /app/backend/tests/ folder — known >240s timeout. Selectors confirmed working: auth-resend-verification-link (login footer), auth-resend-verification-btn (in auth-success-state for register + EMAIL_NOT_VERIFIED). Generic message fragment to assert: 'Si la cuenta existe'. Rate-limit detail fragment: 'Por favor espera'. To bypass the 60s cooldown in tests, seed last_resend_at older than 60s OR use a fresh email each run.
